steps.title: Turn the g table into an f table
steps.content:
The table gives $g(x)$, but the question asks for the y-intercept of $y=f(x)$. Since $g(x)=\dfrac{f(x)}{x+4}$, multiply each table output by $x+4$ to get $f(x)$:
  • $f(-24)=3(-24+4)=-60$
  • $f(-9)=0(-9+4)=0$
  • $f(16)=5(16+4)=100$

steps.title: Use Desmos regression on the derived points
steps.content: Enter the derived $f$ points as $x_1=[-24,-9,16]$ and $y_1=[-60,0,100]$. Then type $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=4$ and $b=36$, so $f(x)=4x+36$.

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is where $x=0$. From $f(x)=4x+36$, the intercept is $(0,36)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
